Class EnhancedLabel

java.lang.Object
javafx.scene.Node
javafx.scene.Parent
javafx.scene.layout.Region
javafx.scene.control.Control
javafx.scene.control.Labeled
javafx.scene.control.Label
com.dlsc.gemsfx.EnhancedLabel
All Implemented Interfaces:
javafx.css.Styleable, javafx.event.EventTarget, javafx.scene.control.Skinnable
public class EnhancedLabel extends javafx.scene.control.Label
An enhanced label that allows for selecting the (whole) label and copying to the clipboard either via keyboard shortcut or via context menu.

    Property
    Description

  • Property Details

    • onCopyAction

      public final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<javafx.event.EventHandler<javafx.event.ActionEvent>> onCopyActionProperty
      The action that is executed when the user copies the label text. The default action is to copy the text to the clipboard. This action can be overridden by setting a new event handler.
      See Also:
      • getOnCopyAction()
      • setOnCopyAction(EventHandler)
      • onCopyActionProperty()

    • selected

      public final javafx.beans.property.BooleanProperty selectedProperty
      Determines whether the label is currently selected or not.
      See Also:
      • isSelected()
      • setSelected(boolean)
      • selectedProperty()

    • copyMenuItemText

      public final javafx.beans.property.StringProperty copyMenuItemTextProperty
      The text used for the "copy" menu item.
      See Also:
      • getCopyMenuItemText()
      • setCopyMenuItemText(String)
      • copyMenuItemTextProperty()

    • copyContentSupplier

      public final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<Supplier<String>> copyContentSupplierProperty
      Stores the supplier used for filling the clipboard when the user copies the label. Sometimes applications want to copy only a part of the label, e.g. an ID shown by the label like this: "Customer account #12345678". The copy content supplier can then decide to just return the ID "12345678" instead of the whole string. The default supplier however returns the whole string.
      See Also:
      • getCopyContentSupplier()
      • setCopyContentSupplier(Supplier)
      • copyContentSupplierProperty()

    • setOnCopyAction

      public final void setOnCopyAction(javafx.event.EventHandler<javafx.event.ActionEvent> value)
      Sets the value of the onCopyAction property.
      Property description:
      The action that is executed when the user copies the label text. The default action is to copy the text to the clipboard. This action can be overridden by setting a new event handler.
      Parameters:
      value - the value for the onCopyAction property
      See Also:
      • getOnCopyAction()
      • onCopyActionProperty()

    • getCopyContentSupplier

      public final Supplier<String> getCopyContentSupplier()
      Gets the value of the copyContentSupplier property.
      Property description:
      Stores the supplier used for filling the clipboard when the user copies the label. Sometimes applications want to copy only a part of the label, e.g. an ID shown by the label like this: "Customer account #12345678". The copy content supplier can then decide to just return the ID "12345678" instead of the whole string. The default supplier however returns the whole string.
      Returns:
      the value of the copyContentSupplier property
      See Also:
      • setCopyContentSupplier(Supplier)
      • copyContentSupplierProperty()

    • copyContentSupplierProperty

      public final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<Supplier<String>> copyContentSupplierProperty()
      Stores the supplier used for filling the clipboard when the user copies the label. Sometimes applications want to copy only a part of the label, e.g. an ID shown by the label like this: "Customer account #12345678". The copy content supplier can then decide to just return the ID "12345678" instead of the whole string. The default supplier however returns the whole string.
      Returns:
      the supplier for the clipboard content when the user copies the label
      See Also:
      • getCopyContentSupplier()
      • setCopyContentSupplier(Supplier)

    • setCopyContentSupplier

      public final void setCopyContentSupplier(Supplier<String> copyContentSupplier)
      Sets the value of the copyContentSupplier property.
      Property description:
      Stores the supplier used for filling the clipboard when the user copies the label. Sometimes applications want to copy only a part of the label, e.g. an ID shown by the label like this: "Customer account #12345678". The copy content supplier can then decide to just return the ID "12345678" instead of the whole string. The default supplier however returns the whole string.
      Parameters:
      copyContentSupplier - the value for the copyContentSupplier property
      See Also:
      • getCopyContentSupplier()
      • copyContentSupplierProperty()
